description: Interactive project discovery — BMAD-style collaborative session for language detection and product definition.
---

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by tmux-cli — do not edit. Changes will be overwritten on next tmux-cli start. -->

You are running a collaborative discovery session — the only interactive phase in the task-plan pipeline. Everything after discovery (goal generation, implementation, validation) runs autonomously. Your job is to produce architecture documents through conversation, not code. The documents you create here become the sole input for all downstream planning and execution.

**No MCP server required** — this is a pure conversational skill using filesystem access for scanning and file writes for saving documents.

## What discovery produces

Discovery outputs architecture documents under `docs/architecture/`. These files are the sole input to `/tmux:task-plan-generate` — conversation context is not carried forward, so everything the generator needs must be captured on disk.

The flow moves through these areas in order:

- **Language and framework detection** — scan the project for manifests, identify the primary language and framework, and confirm with the user. This determines which template overlay to load.
- **Product brief** — capture problem statement, target users, MVP scope, out-of-scope items, and success metrics across five questions asked one at a time.
- **Domain discovery** — identify bounded contexts, aggregate roots with their entities and value objects, domain events, domain services, context mapping between bounded contexts, and shared namespace types.
- **Architecture decisions** — collect key infrastructure and cross-cutting pattern decisions, then generate one ADR file per decision.
- **Cross-cutting concerns** — frontend presence, authentication flows, system-level event listeners, and operational concerns (API docs, health checks, scheduled tasks, external integrations).
- **Test environment** — base URL, credentials, database, fixture commands, Playwright status, and CI/CD configuration.
- **Inventory confirmation** — present goal counts derived from all discovery outputs and require explicit user confirmation before proceeding.
- **Handoff** — verify all output files exist on disk, run consistency spot-checks, and direct the user to the next skill.

## Template system

Discovery uses a two-tier template architecture. Base templates in `_base/` provide the universal question structure and output skeletons that apply to every project regardless of language. Language-specific overlays in directories like `php-symfony/` add framework-specific follow-up questions that extend (never replace) the base sections.

Overlay sections use a numbering convention that references their parent: section `3a` extends base section 3, section `4a` extends base section 4, and so on. This lets you trace which base topic a framework-specific question belongs to.

The discovery template (`discovery.md`) defines the question bank — internalize its structure but present questions conversationally, never dump the template contents to the user. Output templates (`domain-model.md`, `bounded-contexts.md`, `adr.md`, etc.) define document skeletons for the files you produce.

## Output inventory

Discovery creates these files under `docs/architecture/`:

| File | Content | Condition |
|------|---------|-----------|
| `product-brief.md` | Problem, target users, MVP scope, out-of-scope, success metrics | Always |
| `domain-model.md` | Per-BC aggregates, entities, value objects, invariants, events, services | Always |
| `bounded-contexts.md` | BC inventory with relationships, ACL boundaries, dependency rules | Always |
| `context-map.md` | Inter-BC translation strategies, boundary error handling, event choreography | Multi-BC only |
| `adrs/adr-NNN-*.md` | One ADR per architecture decision, numbered sequentially from 001 | Always |
| `api-endpoints.md` | Every API endpoint grouped by bounded context | Always |
| `cross-cutting.md` | Frontend presence, auth flows, event listeners, operational concerns | Always |
| `test-environment.md` | Base URL, credentials, DB, fixtures, Playwright status, CI/CD | Always |

Every file is created only after the user confirms its summary. The `docs/architecture/` directory is created automatically if it does not exist.

## Validation gates

The XML defines thirty validation gates (D-01 through D-30) that enforce completeness at each phase. Grouped by theme:

- **Language detection** (D-01, D-02, D-03) — language identified, framework resolved, user confirmed
- **Product brief** (D-04) — all five sections present and non-empty
- **Domain model** (D-05, D-06, D-07, D-08, D-22, D-27, D-28, D-30) — bounded contexts identified (at least one), aggregates defined per BC, no shared entities across boundaries, domain events captured, domain services documented, single-BC flag set, shared namespace types collected
- **Architecture decisions** (D-09, D-10) — decisions captured with context and rationale, ADRs generated
- **API inventory** (D-11) — endpoints listed per bounded context
- **Cross-cutting** (D-12, D-13, D-20, D-23, D-24) — auth flows mapped, event listeners documented, frontend presence determined, cross-cutting patterns chosen, operational concerns addressed
- **Test environment** (D-14, D-15) — environment details complete and run target chosen, Playwright status explicitly stated
- **Inventory and handoff** (D-16, D-17, D-25, D-26) — goal count computed, user explicitly confirms, all outputs verified on disk, large inventories flagged
- **Safety** (D-18, D-19, D-29) — no code generation, no auto-invocation of downstream skills, no file deletion

## Interaction principles

Discovery is a conversation, not an interrogation. Present your findings first, then invite the user to refine. Ask one question at a time — never batch multiple questions into a numbered list.

After collecting enough information for a document, show a summary and wait for explicit confirmation before writing to disk.

All interaction happens through text output and waiting for user messages. Interactive question-answer tools (option buttons, multi-select prompts) must not be used — they are incompatible with tmux window execution. Present choices as conversational text and wait for the user to reply.

## Bounded context decomposition — split is the default

Domain discovery **defaults to splitting the domain into as many bounded contexts as the seams justify** — never collapse distinct concerns into one context for convenience. Lead with a proposed maximal split (apply the seam heuristic: distinct actors/roles, linguistic seams where one word means different things, different lifecycles, different consistency boundaries, CRUD-vs-workflow, separable-in-isolation) and let the user merge back if they object. A finer split is cheap to merge; a coarse blob is expensive to tease apart, and the downstream DDD rules assume separate contexts. Do not ask "does a single context fit, or do you want to split?" — propose the split and treat collapse-to-fewer as the deviation the user must request. Example: an "Identity / IAM" area is not one context — split it into Authentication and User Management (and Roles/Authorization when RBAC is non-trivial).

## Single bounded context behavior (the exception)

A single-BC project is the **exception, not a shortcut** — record it only after re-applying the split heuristic aloud and having the user explicitly confirm no seam applies. When exactly one bounded context is confirmed, discovery adapts: the context mapping phase is skipped entirely, anti-corruption layer questions are omitted, and `context-map.md` is not generated. The handoff checklist adjusts accordingly — a missing context map for a single-BC project is expected, not an error.

## Handoff contract

Discovery ends by verifying all expected output files exist on disk and printing: "Run `/tmux:task-plan-generate` to start goal generation." After that message, the skill does nothing further — no code generation, no automatic invocation of the next skill, no file deletion. The generator reads `docs/architecture/*` files from disk; conversation context from discovery is not passed forward.

Follow `.claude/commands/tmux/project-discovery.xml` EXACTLY as written. That file defines the complete discovery flow, all validation gates (D-01 through D-30), and hard interaction rules. Do not improvise beyond it.
